title: Work Grill skill compatibility with the framework structure
problem_statement: The framework's Lifecycle depends on three global skills — grill-with-docs, to-prd, to-issues — that assume a different methodology: an external issue tracker with a triage-label vocabulary, docs/adr/ decision records, and file layouts our Instances don't have. Every invocation forces the agent to translate ("publish to the tracker" → we mean issues.json), and the framework's no-ADR rule swims upstream against tooling that assumes ADRs exist. The Setup Grill also starts its philosophy question from a blank page even when the target codebase already practices philosophies it could propose.
solution: Make the Work Grill native to the framework. Restore ADRs as design-time decision records mapped to the framework's own structure. Catalog every deviation between the three skills and the framework empirically — by invoking them against a real Instance — then resolve each skill (fork into the framework, or adapt our structure) until a full Work Grill runs with zero translation glue. Add a philosophy-scan so the Setup Grill proposes philosophies discovered in the codebase instead of asking cold.

user_stories.actor: Team Lead setting up a new project
user_stories.want: the Setup Grill to scan my codebase for existing philosophy signals before asking
user_stories.benefit: the grill proposes rather than starts blank

user_stories.want: philosophy candidates presented as confirmable proposals
user_stories.benefit: grilling is fast and grounded in what the codebase already practices

user_stories.want: hard-to-reverse decisions recorded at grill time as ADRs
user_stories.benefit: design rationale is not deferred to sprint close

user_stories.want: ADRs colocated with the methodology docs
user_stories.benefit: one namespace holds the project's durable memory

user_stories.want: the decision-record split defined — ADR at design time, retro at sprint close
user_stories.benefit: I log decisions in the right place without judgment calls

user_stories.want: Principles and PROCESS updated to the restored-ADR policy
user_stories.benefit: my rubric does not fail valid ADR work

user_stories.actor: main-session agent running the Work Grill
user_stories.want: grill-with-docs to write decision records to the framework's location
user_stories.benefit: I never translate between two structures

user_stories.want: the publish target defined by the framework
user_stories.benefit: "publish to the tracker" needs no interpretation

user_stories.want: issues written to the framework's issues.json in schema
each skill's deviations cataloged as issues before changing anything

user_stories.benefit: we do not blanket-fork generic skills that mostly work

user_stories.want: Preflight to hard-gate on the final skill manifest
user_stories.benefit: setup reflects what the framework actually uses

user_stories.want: the Work Grill to run end-to-end with zero translation glue
user_stories.benefit: the framework feels native rather than bolted on

user_stories.want: every "no ADRs" statement removed in one sweep
user_stories.benefit: the docs never contradict the policy

implementation_decisions.title: ADRs are restored
implementation_decisions.summary: Design-time decision records; the retro remains the sprint-time record. Offer criteria stay grill-with-docs's three: hard to reverse, surprising without context, result of a real trade-off.

implementation_decisions.title: ADRs map to the framework's structure, not docs/adr/
implementation_decisions.summary: They live in the methodology namespace; the exact location resolves together with EXEC-001. ADRs are committed prose, one decision per numbered file.

implementation_decisions.title: Philosophy-scan
implementation_decisions.summary: The Setup Grill spawns a read-only subagent to sweep the target codebase (readmes, contributor docs, configs, prior documentation) and return philosophy candidates. Candidates are proposals the user confirms; the scan never writes PHILOSOPHY.md itself.

implementation_decisions.title: Deviation catalog precedes any skill change
implementation_decisions.summary: Each mismatch found while invoking a skill becomes one backlog issue. The catalog from this PRD's own production run seeds it.

implementation_decisions.title: Per-skill resolution
implementation_decisions.summary: Decided from the catalog: fork the skill into the framework, or adapt the framework's structure. The acceptance bar for either: zero translation language at point of use.

implementation_decisions.title: Preflight and the Scaffolder template update last
implementation_decisions.summary: To match the resolution outcome: manifest of required skills, stamped references to the ADR location.

implementation_decisions.title: All "no ADRs" statements are removed
implementation_decisions.summary: From the Principles, PROCESS, Lifecycle glossary entry, and Team Directive as part of ADR restoration — Src-First, alignment-gated.

implementation_decisions.title: Tracker mapping for this framework
implementation_decisions.summary: PRDs and issues are schema-validated JSON in the work-tracking area (prds/, issues.json) — the adherence-vs-understanding line: artifacts agents execute against are JSON; documents humans read for context (including ADRs) are markdown. No label vocabulary — backlog entry plus actionable_now is the triage signal.

testing_decisions: A good test observes external behavior: invoke the skill, inspect the artifacts it produces. Never assert on prompt internals.
package-qa continues to gate every Scaffolder change (stamp manifest, idempotency, JSON parse, gitignore, skip-safety).

Acceptance test for the whole PRD: one clean end-to-end Work Grill (grill → PRD → issues) on a scratch Instance with zero translation language. Prior art: this PRD's own production run, which is the first deviation data point.
out_of_scope: Executing EXEC-001 (the namespace move itself) — the ADR location depends on its outcome, but the move is its own issue.
Review of make-teammate and async-questions (not Work Grill skills).
npm publishing and global skill installation mechanics (PRD-002's domain).
notes: Deviation catalog seeded by the to-prd run (2026-06-07): (1) to-prd expects an issue tracker + triage-label vocabulary provided by /setup-matt-pocock-skills — the framework has neither. (2) to-prd applies a needs-triage label — the framework has no labels; triage is the Team Lead pulling backlog issues into a sprint. (3) to-prd says 'respect any ADRs' — second skill to assume ADRs; evidence used in the restore decision. (4) to-prd's module/testing framing assumes a code feature — framework deliverables are mostly prompt artifacts, testable only by invocation. (5) grill-with-docs hardcodes docs/adr/ and a CONTEXT-MAP.md convention the framework does not use. (6) to-prd's template forbids file paths in Implementation Decisions — framework artifacts are files; resolved by naming artifacts, not paths.

Deviation catalog, to-issues run (2026-06-07): (7) same tracker/label/setup-skill assumption. (8) third 'respect ADRs' occurrence. (9) vertical-slice rules assume code layers (schema/API/UI/tests). (10) issue-template fields (Parent, Acceptance criteria, slice type) had no schema home — closed by this PRD's EXEC-004.

Amendment 2026-06-07: first published as markdown; converted to this JSON form under EXEC-004 the same day. Original tracker-mapping decision said 'PRDs are prose markdown' — corrected by Team Lead ruling: PRDs and issues are JSON (adherence-vs-understanding line).
